questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) over Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs) for prototyping or low-volume production?
options: Lower unit cost for high-volume production.
Higher clock frequencies due to fixed, optimized routing.
Flexibility and reconfigurability after manufacturing, allowing design changes.
Significantly lower static power consumption per functional unit.
Smaller silicon footprint for the same functional complexity.

questions.question: In the context of computer architecture, which of the following best describes the primary goal of a "cache coherence protocol" in a multi-processor system?
options: To ensure that all caches store identical data to the main memory at all times.
To prevent deadlocks when multiple processors attempt to acquire shared locks.
To maintain a consistent view of shared memory across multiple processors' private caches.
To optimize the paging process for virtual memory systems by reducing TLB misses.
To reduce the number of instructions executed per cycle by enabling speculative execution.

questions.question: Which of the following statements accurately describes the fundamental difference between a "Hard IP" and a "Soft IP" core in ASIC design?
options: Hard IP is exclusively used for analog functions, while Soft IP is only for digital logic.
Hard IP is delivered as a technology-specific, fully laid-out GDSII block, whereas Soft IP is delivered as synthesizable RTL code.
Soft IP requires a separate license for each individual chip produced, while Hard IP is typically a one-time purchase.
Hard IP is primarily designed for high-performance applications, while Soft IP is optimized for low-power consumption.
Soft IP typically includes embedded processors, whereas Hard IP mainly refers to memory blocks like SRAM or ROM.

questions.question: During the physical verification stage of an Integrated Circuit (IC) design, what is the primary purpose of a "Layout Versus Schematic (LVS)" check?
options: To verify that all transistors are properly sized for optimal performance and power.
To ensure that the physical layout adheres strictly to the manufacturing process design rules (e.g., minimum widths and spacing).
To compare the extracted netlist from the physical layout against the original golden netlist (from RTL or gate-level schematic) to confirm topological and functional equivalence.
To identify and rectify dynamic timing violations caused by variations in signal propagation delays.
To automatically generate the final GDSII stream file that will be sent to the foundry for fabrication.

questions.question: In modern processor architectures, what is the primary role of a "Hypervisor" (also known as a Virtual Machine Monitor or VMM) in relation to the CPU?
options: To manage memory paging and virtual address translation for a single guest operating system.
To abstract and control the physical hardware resources, enabling multiple guest operating systems to run concurrently and isolated.
To accelerate specific floating-point arithmetic operations within the CPU's Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU).
To handle interrupt requests and context switching between user-level applications within a single OS.
To optimize the instruction fetching and decoding stages in a deep pipelined processor.
